channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Empathy Adds Enterprise Portal for Life Insurers
item.description: Empathy Connect is an enterprise portal that brings together legacy planning, bereavement support and professional engagement tools into a connected experience for insurance and wealth professionals. Empathy (New York), a technology company focused on planning and support around major life events, has added Empathy Connect to its platform for insurance and wealth management organizations. The company says the addition marks a step in its evolution from care for families delivered through insurance and financial institutions to an enterprise platform that helps organizations support people before, during and after life-changing events.

item.title: ‘Asia is our active strategic focus’, Fidelis deputy chair aims to bulk up property treaty, specialty lines
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: APOLLO introduces free address changes for tenant policies
item.description: Canadian digital insurance provider APOLLO Insurance introduced a new feature allowing tenants to update their address on an existing insurance policy at no additional cost when moving anywhere within Canada. APOLLO+ customers will also retain their renewal pricing following an address change. The update can be completed through APOLLO’s online self service portal without requiring a new policy, premium recalculation, or additional underwriting questions.

item.title: Athora completes CEO succession
item.description: Athora Holding , a European savings and retirement services group, announced the completion of its planned CEO succession, with Todd Solash formally assuming the role of group chief executive officer. The leadership transition follows Athora’s acquisition of Pension Insurance Corporation and a related €3.5 billion common equity rights issue. Following the transaction, Athora said it now manages €139 billion in assets under management and administration and serves more than three million customers across Europe.

item.title: AXA XL promotes Nick Goepp and Lexi Bueno in U.S. Casualty Division
item.description: Casualty business, appointing Nick Goepp as head of West, excess casualty, and Lexi Bueno as head of Central, U.S. The post AXA XL promotes Nick Goepp and Lexi Bueno in U.S. Based in San Francisco, Goepp succeeds Stitham in overseeing the excess casualty West business after Stitham’s promotion to CUO earlier this year.

item.title: John Hancock Launches Longevity Network
item.description: (Image source: John Hancock Longevity Preparedness Index site.) John Hancock (Boston) has launched the Longer, Healthier, Better Network (LBH Network), an exclusive community of forward-thinking independent financial professionals designed to help customers live longer, healthier, better lives. Launching with an initial 200 founding members, the network expands John Hancock’s broader longevity strategy through solutions and services intended to support financial professionals as they help clients grow their businesses, build on John Hancock’s health and financial well-being platform, and deliver more comprehensive insurance and wealth-planning solutions. John Hancock says these trends are putting pressure on financial professionals to help customers prepare for longer lives, including health, longevity and financial well-being considerations.

item.title: Fatalities Reported in Washington Packaging Facility Explosion
item.description: An accident at a Washington state packaging facility has left at least one person dead, nine missing and nine others injured, with crews working to contain a ruptured tank of caustic chemicals. The incident occurred Tuesday morning at Nippon Dynawave Packaging Co.’s facility in Longview, as a massive industrial tank of “white liquor” collapsed, officials said. Officials did not say what caused the explosion, Charlotte-based WBTV reported.

item.title: Supreme Court Rejects Florida’s Bid to Sue Western States Over Immigrant CDLs
item.description: The Supreme Court on Tuesday rejected Florida’s long shot attempt to sue California and Washington state over the issuance of commercial driver licenses to truckers who don’t speak English and are not authorized to be in the United States. Republican-led Florida has accused the Western states, led by Democrats, of openly defying immigration laws and asked the justices to rule that states lack the authority to issue CDLs to people who are not citizens or legal permanent residents. The Supreme Court typically hears appeals of lower-court decisions, but it sometimes takes on what are known as original lawsuits in which states sue each other in the nation’s highest court.

item.title: NTSB Calls for FAA Revision of Runway Safety Assessments During Heavy Rainfall
item.description: National Transportation Safety Board on Tuesday called on the Federal Aviation Administration to revise how it assesses runway conditions during heavy rainfall, citing safety risks of airplanes skidding off the runway. The NTSB also recommended the FAA issue new rainfall intensity descriptors for aviation weather reports to identify rainfall intensities exceeding the current heavy rain threshold of 0.3 inch (7.62 mm) per hour. The NTSB said wheel braking friction calculations for wet runways can be substantially less than those specified by FAA runway condition assessments due to limitations in the factors considered.

item.title: Modernized Federal Tool Aims to Reduce CDL Fraud
item.description: A new tool launched by the federal government aims to improve road safety by modernizing commercial drivers’ registration. DOT Registration System, replaces what the Trump administration calls “a decades-old network of loosely connected applications rife with fraud, waste, and abuse.” Leveraging biometrics and data analytics, the new anti-fraud registration system promises to ensure only qualified drivers are approved to operate on U.S. In the past, the Trump administration said that “bad actors applying for a federal trucking registration number could easily hide their identity, game the system, and endanger American families on the road.

item.title: Buckle and subsidiary Gateway sue China Construction Bank over Vesttoo LOC fraud
item.description: Buckle, now a fronting specialist for MGA programs and previously a rideshare and gig insurance company, has become the latest to reveal issues caused by fraudulent reinsurance letters of credit linked to Vesttoo and is now suing the purported LOC provider China Construction Bank for damages. The court action arose as Buckle claims China Construction Bank (CCB) failed to honour the LOCs, that were supposed to have collateralized reinsurance obligations owed to Gateway. Nine letters of credit are the subject of Buckle’s lawsuit in an Illinois court against China Construction Bank entities, with the plaintiff claiming the bank had signed off on $9,799,895 of collateral across the nine LOCs.

item.title: India’s Prized Alphonso Mango Crop Ruined by Weather
item.description: Maharashtra is renowned for its Alphonso mangoes, but officials say hotter weather has ruined this year’s crop of the variety known as the “King of Mangoes.” A sharp difference in day and nighttime temperatures in December and January hurt flowering and fruit setting, while hotter than usual weather in April and May, probably due to the El Niño weather phenomenon, then spoiled the fruits themselves, said Bapusaheb Manikrao Lambade, a government agriculture officer in Devgad, one of Maharashtra’s top Alphonso-growing areas. In India’s western state of Maharashtra, mango farmer Komal Walke is scrambling to meet orders from India’s online grocers after her family’s three acres of orchards produced almost no Alphonso mangoes this year. India’s entire mango crop was worth $2.3 billion last year, according to Indian research firm Mordor Intelligence, which expects the market to grow to $3.4 billion by 2031.

item.title: Northwestern Medicine Agrees to Pay $325K in Religious Discrimination Case
item.description: Northwestern Medicine agreed to pay $325,000 in monetary relief to a class of employees denied vaccine exemptions because of their religious beliefs or practices and to provide other relief following an investigation by the U.S. The agreement resolves charges filed with the EEOC alleging discrimination against employees based on their religion when it denied reasonable accommodation requests to employees who requested a faith-based exemption to Northwestern Medicine’s mandatory flu vaccination policy. The EEOC’s investigation found that Northwestern Medicine discriminated against a class of employees from November 10, 2023, to the present at its facilities across Illinois by denying these employees a religious accommodation and by denying them the opportunity to earn an annual bonus which the employer described as a “vaccine incentive bonus,” intended to motivate compliance with the vaccination mandate policy.

item.title: 4 Arrested in Louisiana Insurance Fraud Scheme
item.description: The Louisiana State Police Insurance Fraud / Auto Theft Unit (IFAT) has arrested four individuals following an investigation into an alleged automobile insurance fraud scheme in Iberia Parish The investigation began after a criminal referral from Timber Creek Insurance Services and the Louisiana Department of Insurance Fraud Division regarding allegations of fraudulent insurance claims involving 29-year-old Rayshonte Oppenheimer, 32-year-old Briannie Butler, 24-year-old Passion Keal, and 33-year-old Quandalyn Bernard, all of New Iberia. Between April 30 and May 1, 2026, investigators obtained arrest warrants for all four suspects for Automobile Insurance Fraud (Felony). The investigation determined the suspects made false statements during the insurance claims process following a 2025 crash involving a Pelican Waste & Debris garbage truck.

item.title: Pace raises $46m Series B to expand AI operations platform for insurers
item.description: Pace has raised $46 million in Series B funding, co-led by Thrive Capital and Sequoia Capital, with participation from Emergence Capital and Pruven Capital, as the AI operations company accelerates expansion across global insurance markets. Founded last year, Pace develops AI agents designed specifically for insurance workflows spanning submissions, renewals, policy servicing and claims operations. According to Pace, the platform is being used to automate thousands of hours of customer acquisition and policy servicing work at Prudential.

item.title: Carriers Face Retention Problem
item.description: Record insurance shopping driven by economic stress forces carriers to shift from reactive pricing tactics to proactive retention strategies. This puts carriers in a race to understand—and hopefully prevent or at least forestall—what looks like a retention crisis. Gas prices have shot up—and face further surges as politically volatile oil-producing regions continue to roil.

item.title: How Carriers Can Move From AI Pilot to Business Transformation
item.description: Here are where many projects fail and how carriers can move AI projects onward to implementation and business transformation. James Thom, Chief Product Officer at Vertafore, said at an InsurTech Summit panel that a focus on outcomes can help many carriers move on from the pilot stage. How to move AI projects past the pilot stage To break out of the cycle of experimentation, carriers should ask where an AI tool can create measurable ROI and then move forward aggressively.

item.title: GenStar introduces PEO insurance program
item.description: GenStar Insurance Services launched STAFFprotect+, a new excess and surplus insurance program for professional employer organizations and staffing agencies offered through Starfish Specialty Insurance . Starfish Specialty brings strong program administration capabilities and market insight, and together we believe this offering delivers a competitive, well-structured solution for PEOs and staffing agencies seeking broad, reliable coverage.” – Matt Brown, Senior Vice President and Delegated Division Manager for GenStar “A comprehensive suite of insurance solutions serving the PEO and staffing industries has been lacking for some time. STAFFprotect+ is our second program in this segment, and we couldn’t be more excited to be launching it with GenStar.” – Jeremy Hitzig, CEO of Starfish Specialty Insurance.

item.title: Pouch Insurance partners with First Acceptance Corporation
item.description: Commercial auto insurance startup Pouch Insurance announced the launch of its Micro-Fleet Rideshare program, an admitted commercial auto product underwritten by First Acceptance Insurance Company of Tennessee, a subsidiary of First Acceptance Corporation. Partnering with First Acceptance to bring this product to market is a significant moment for us – and for every fleet owner in Tennessee who has been trying to find a real solution to this problem.” – Steve McKay, Chief Executive Officer of Pouch Insurance. We are genuinely excited about what we are building together.” – Anthony Delaney, President of First Acceptance Insurance Company of Tennessee.

item.title: Florida Court Says 2020 Law Gives ‘Very Broad’ Liability Immunity to Rideshare Firms
item.description: A Florida appeals court this month handed ridesharing companies like Uber and Lyft a major legal victory with a broad interpretation of what the court called an unusually broad liability immunity law. “The scope of immunity provided by Subsection 18’s plain text is very broad. Whether it is wise to confer such broad immunity is not a question for this branch to answer,” Judge Jonathan Lott wrote for the appeals court.

item.title: Storm Counts, Landfalls and Losses: The Hidden Risk Behind a ‘Quiet’ Hurricane Season
item.description: Here, Verisk meteorology and scientific risk modeling experts Tory Farney and Wesley Terwey unpack why storm counts, landfalls and insured losses often diverge, and what last year's season can reveal about hurricane risk heading into the 2026 season. Some observers may label the 2025 Atlantic hurricane season as a “quiet” year due to zero U.S. hurricane landfalls, but from an insurance and catastrophe risk perspective, that conclusion misses the point—and, potentially, the risk.

item.title: Deutsche Rück flags Myanmar earthquake as biggest loss in 2025 as profits jump 45%
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: South Korean financial regulator approves Lotte’s management improvement plan: reports
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Vacancies open for FCA Smaller Business Practitioner Panel
item.description: The Smaller Business Practitioner Panel provides independent advice and challenge from the perspective of smaller firms, helping to shape our work at a time of significant change in UK financial services regulation. We’re inviting applications from senior practitioners at smaller regulated firms in the general insurance and consumer credit sectors to join the panel. Its key remit is to provide input to the FCA from the industry to help us meet our strategic and operational objectives from a smaller business standpoint.

item.title: Review of financial promotion approvers finds some firms need to raise standards
item.description: Firms that approve financial promotions should be doing more to protect consumers, an FCA review has found. 'Firms must make sure every promotion they sign off is fair, clear and not misleading.' As a result of the FCA's work, one firm has already had to conduct a remediation exercise and some websites have been blocked to retail customers. However, the FCA also found that some firms approved adverts with unsubstantiated claims or allowed retail investors to see promotions intended for professional clients.

item.title: The Savvy Logic Behind VC Bets In ‘Uninvestable’ Sectors
item.description: Software juggernauts serving heavy industries — IBM , SAP , ServiceNow , Schneider Electric — have gone from safe bets to being the subject of investor scrutiny. Defense, energy, robotics and government have historically been classic no-go areas for VC investment. These “hard” industries have slow procurement cycles, tight regulatory oversight and high-friction customer migration in common.

item.title: Exclusive: Capchase, The ‘Affirm for B2B,’ Secures $200M In Debt And Equity
item.description: Financing startup Capchase has secured a new round of funding, consisting of $26 million in equity and a $174 million credit facility, the company told Crunchbase News exclusively. Founded in 2020, New York-based Capchase initially made a name for itself by providing revenue-based financing for SaaS companies . Capchase embeds itself directly into the sales workflows of companies such as original equipment manufacturers, software vendors and cybersecurity providers.

item.title: Korean Re secures targeted $75m multi-peril retro through second Solomon Re cat bond
item.description: Korean Re, the global reinsurance company headquartered in the country of its name, has now secured the targeted $75 million of multi-peril retrocession from its Solomon Re Ltd. Nearly three years ago, Korean Re sponsored its debut catastrophe bond, securing $75 million in US multi-peril catastrophe retrocession from its first Solomon Re 2023-1 issuance. As we previously explained, while the first Solomon Re cat bond only covered US named storm and earthquake risks for Korean Re, through this Solomon Re Series 2026-1 issuance, Korean Re will receive a source of retrocession for Israel earthquake risks, which is a peril rarely seen in the market.

item.title: Mercury targets $100m California fire reinsurance with second Luca Re catastrophe bond
item.description: Mercury General Corporation (Mercury Insurance), the California headquartered insurer, is back in the catastrophe bond market seeking additional fire reinsurance protection, with an initial $100 million target for its second 144A cat bond sponsorship of a Luca Re Ltd. Mercury Insurance sponsored its first 144A catastrophe bond last year, securing $150 million in California fire reinsurance protection from the capital markets with its debut Luca Re 2025-1 deal. Having secured $150 million of California wildfire and fire following earthquake protection from the capital markets with its first Luca Re cat bond a year ago, we’ve learned that Mercury is now back and looking to extend that reinsurance with its second 144A cat bond sponsorship.

item.title: Oak raises target size of Quercian Re 2026-1 cat bond, now seeks up to $150m
item.description: Oak Global, the specialist Lloyd’s underwriting company, has now raised the target size for its debut catastrophe bond sponsorship, with retrocessional protection from the Arthur Re Ltd. – Quercian Re 2026-1 cat bond issuance now expected to fall between $125 million and as much as $150 million, Artemis understands. Oak Global made its debut in the catastrophe bond market earlier this month, with this Quercian Re 2026-1 issuance set to become the first catastrophe bond that will benefit Oak Reinsurance Syndicate 2843, a syndicate focused on reinsurance opportunities that is operated by specialist underwriter in the Lloyd’s market Oak Global.

item.title: Kemper names Stephen McAnena as President & CEO
item.description: US-based insurer Kemper Corporation has announced the appointment of Stephen J. McAnena as President and Chief Executive Officer (CEO), and will also join its Board of Directors, effective 1st June 2026. McAnena will also join the company’s Board of Directors on the same date.

item.title: Canada Life agrees £36m pension buy-in with HSB Engineering Insurance Services scheme
item.description: Canada Life, a UK insurer providing life insurance and pension risk transfer services, has completed a £36 million full-scheme buy-in with the pension scheme of HSB Engineering Insurance Services Limited. The sponsoring employer, HSB UK & Ireland, part of a UK underwriting business specialising in engineering and technology insurance and inspection services across construction, manufacturing, leisure, retail and commercial sectors as well as public services, supported the arrangement alongside Canada Life. The buy-in forms part of ongoing pension risk transfer activity in the UK, with Canada Life working with the scheme and its advisers to complete the agreement in a coordinated process involving all parties.

item.title: Liberty Specialty Markets names Monique Kooijman as General Manager for Netherlands, Belgium and Nordics
item.description: Liberty Specialty Markets, the commercial and specialty insurance division of Liberty Mutual Insurance Group, has announced the appointment of Monique Kooijman as General Manager for the Netherlands, Belgium and the Nordics, subject to regulatory approval. I am delighted to announce the appointment of Monique for General Manager across Netherlands, Belgium and the Nordics – all regions we are investing in as we execute our Invest in Europe strategy and drive towards our 2030 ambitions.” Liberty Specialty Markets noted that, while at CNA Hardy, Kooijman served as Regional Director with oversight of the Benelux countries, Germany and the Nordics.

item.title: OpenAI’s Altman Says AI Unlikely to Lead to ‘Jobs Apocalypse’
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman said on Tuesday the rapid development and adoption of AI would not lead to a global “jobs apocalypse” and the technology had not claimed as many white-collar jobs as he had feared. “I’m delighted to be wrong about this, I thought there would have been more impact on entry-level white-collar jobs being eliminated by now than has actually happened,” Altman told CBA Chief Executive Matt Comyn in an interview. “People are like ‘oh you could have saved the world a lot of fear mongering and a lot of doom and gloom’ but at the time I was like ‘I see this is a real risk we should probably talk about it’ and it still may.” Altman did not cite any jobs numbers on Tuesday but has previously talked about potential industry-wide job cuts due to AI’s advancement.

item.title: California Officials Lift Evacuation Orders for Residents Near Damaged Chemical Tank
item.description: Southern California officials lifted the final evacuation orders Tuesday night for residents who live near a damaged chemical tank, allowing 16,000 people to return home. “All residents will go home,” Orange County Fire Authority Division Chief Craig Covey said during a meeting that grew raucous at times as residents questioned why the chemical was allowed in a densely populated area and urged city officials to hold the company that operates the tank accountable. Authorities announced they were lifting the final orders after the temperature on the tank remained stable for four hours without intervention from sprinklers.

item.title: Fatalities Reported in Washington State Packaging Facility Explosion
item.description: An accident at a Washington state packaging facility has left at least one person dead, nine missing and nine others injured, with crews working to contain a ruptured tank of caustic chemicals. The incident occurred Tuesday morning at Nippon Dynawave Packaging Co.’s facility in Longview, as a massive industrial tank of “white liquor” collapsed, officials said. Officials did not say what caused the explosion, Charlotte-based WBTV reported.

item.title: FutureProof launches E&S condo and renters program
item.description: Climate risk MGA FutureProof Technologies launched a new excess and surplus personal lines insurance program targeting condo and renters policies in catastrophe-exposed areas across the Southeast, including Florida and Texas. “Condo and renters policyholders in catastrophe-prone states like Florida and Texas are among the most underserved in the country. Their ability to deliver instant, AI-priced quotes on condo and renters coverage in Florida and Texas gives our retail brokers a competitive edge in markets where finding quality coverage can be challenging.

item.title: John Hancock launches longevity-focused advisor network
item.description: The network launches with 200 founding members and builds on John Hancock’s Vitality Program, which combines life insurance with wellness incentives and health engagement tools. Network, a community of independent financial professionals focused on integrating longevity, wellness, and financial protection into client planning. John Hancock said the initiative is designed to address growing consumer interest in longevity and wellness as the U.S.

item.title: Safepoint launches IPO
item.description: Safepoint announced the launch of its initial public offering of 16.7 million shares of common stock, with an expected price range of $15 to $17 per share. The company plans to list on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol “SFPT.” The Tampa-based company operates as a specialty homeowners and commercial insurance underwriter managing reciprocal exchanges Manatee Insurance Exchange and Cajun Underwriters Reciprocal Exchange, alongside Safepoint Insurance Company. The offering includes approximately 6.2 million shares sold by Safepoint Holdings and about 10.4 million shares offered by existing shareholders.

item.title: onLygal incorpora a Rubén Gómez como delegado territorial para la zona Centro
item.description: onLygal ha anunciado el nombramiento de Rubén Gómez Gismera como delegado territorial para la zona Centro, asumiendo también la responsabilidad de alianzas estratégicas en el Canal Mediado . En este sentido, reportará a Ramón Gómez, director comercial del Canal Mediado. Licenciado en Economía y en Ciencias Actuariales y Financieras por la Universidad Carlos III de Madrid, con postgrado en Ahorro y Previsión por la UPF Barcelona School of Management y formación como corredor de seguros, aporta más de 20 años de experiencia en el sector asegurador , con una trayectoria tanto en gestión técnica como en dirección comercial y desarrollo de alianzas con aseguradoras y empresas tecnológicas.

item.title: Guillermo Hoces se une a Munich Re Specialty como country claims manager
item.description: Como anunció ayer a través de su perfil de LinkedIn Gianluca Piscopo, managing director, la oficina de Munich Re Specialty Madrid ha reforzado su equipo de Reclamaciones con Guillermo Hoces Hurtado como country claims manager. Procede de Everest , donde llevaba algo más de dos años y donde hasta ahora ocupaba el cargo de deputy head of claims Continental Europe. Licenciado en Derecho y Economía, lleva más de 15 años de trayectoria profesional en el sector , en el que también ha trabajado en Axa XL, XL Catlin y Asemas.

item.title: Allianz y WWF invertirán 270.000 euros en restaurar el río Mijares tras la DANA
item.description: Allianz y WWF han puesto en marcha un proyecto de restauración ecológica en el entorno del Río Mijares, entre los municipios valencianos de Buñol y Yátova, con el objetivo de recuperar los ecosistemas afectados por la DANA de octubre de 2024 y reforzar la resiliencia del territorio frente a futuros episodios climáticos extremos. La iniciativa contará con una inversión de 270.000 euros durante los próximos tres años y contempla actuaciones sobre el cauce y la vegetación de ribera para mejorar la capacidad natural del río de absorber crecidas y reducir el impacto de posibles inundaciones. Entre las medidas previstas figuran la eliminación de especies invasoras, la limpieza selectiva del cauce, la instalación de estructuras naturales para reducir la fuerza del agua y la reforestación con especies autóctonas.

item.title: State Regulatory Surge, Federal Shifts Reshaping Workers’ Comp
item.description: Workers’ compensation is entering a period of heightened complexity as regulatory changes and policy shifts continue to unfold across federal and state levels. Although workers’ comp is governed at the state level, federal developments continue to influence the broader property/casualty landscape in meaningful ways. So far this year, a surge in legislative activity, combined with evolving federal healthcare policy and persistent medical cost pressures, is reshaping the operating environment.

item.title: Insured losses from late-November storms in Australia trimmed: Perils
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Michael Hannigan returns to AIG in Australia
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Allstate returns some Sanders 2022-1 aggregate cat bond principal to investors, extends rest
item.description: Allstate has returned some principal from one of its outstanding aggregate catastrophe bonds to investors, while extending the maturity on the remaining amount, having ended the last annual aggregate risk period that its cat bonds were subject to with total pre-tax reported catastrophe losses just shy of $4 billion. We don’t know what the qualifying loss accumulation was against these Sanders Re III 2022-1 Class C notes, but we’ve now learned that at the end of the annual risk period some of their principal was returned to investors, with the remainder now having an extended maturity date. Recall that Allstate’s annual aggregate risk period for its nationwide (ex-Florida) catastrophe bond backed reinsurance protection runs from April 1st each year.

item.title: The return of “Humpty Dumpty” as Florida reinsurance conditions ease?
item.description: As reinsurance market conditions ease meaningfully for Florida based insurers at the mid-year renewals, an analyst report has cited American Integrity CEO Bob Ritchie as saying the easing is around more than just price, with improvements in terms and conditions suggesting “Humpty Dumpty is getting put back together.” TD Cowen analysts met with a number of insurance market participants in the Tampa Bay area recently, concluding that the Florida market is loosening at primary and reinsurance levels, while hearing of irrational behaviour from some newer market participants. Reinsurance market dynamics have always been a driver for competitive tendencies in Florida’s insurance market and with more pronounced price softening being seen at these reinsurance renewals, with rates said down 15% +, it’s leading to weaker terms and conditions, the analysts said. American Integrity Insurance CEO Bob Ritchie told the analysts that “Humpty Dumpty is getting put back together,” as reinsurance coverage options improve for protection buyers.

item.title: Addresscloud adds Skyblu’s high-resolution European wildfire model
item.description: Addresscloud, a UK geocoding and property intelligence expert, has adopted the high-resolution, pan-European wildfire risk model developed by Skyblu, an AI solutions provider. Skyblu’s high-resolution new model delivers 20-metre resolution risk scoring across Europe, offering significantly greater granularity than traditional datasets. The pair said that this partnership reflects growing demand for high-resolution climate intelligence in underwriting, with both companies already supporting wind and hail risk modelling in selected markets such as France and Spain, and planning a broader European rollout.

item.title: Weak cyber hygiene among smaller businesses exposes protection gap: KYND
item.description: Smaller businesses exhibit widespread weaknesses in cyber hygiene, including a lack of basic email security protections, highlighting cyber exposure and a significant opportunity for insurers to support improved resilience, according to Ben Duffy, VP and Head of North America at KYND. Cyber risk intelligence provider KYND has released research based on 7,980 small and medium-sized businesses (SMBs) across the US and Canada, and 830 UK small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs). Despite this, cyber insurance penetration among SMEs and SMBs remains relatively low — often estimated at below 10% in many segments — highlighting a substantial gap between cyber exposure and protection.

item.title: SiriusPoint names Paul Beattie and Ed Winter as Heads of new Crisis Solutions platform
item.description: SiriusPoint, a specialty insurer and reinsurer, has appointed Paul Beattie and Ed Winter as joint Heads of its newly established Crisis Solutions class of business within its London Market Specialty division. Bringing them together is a defining moment for both the market and SiriusPoint, giving us the scale, expertise and credibility from day one to build a disciplined, durable portfolio.” David Govrin, Group President and Chief Executive Officer, Global Reinsurance & London Market Specialty, SiriusPoint, said, “The launch of Crisis Solutions and the hiring of Ed and Paul shortly after the establishment of our London Market Specialty division reflect our ambition to grow our position in the specialty classes where global clients have always sought the innovation, product expertise, and capacity of the London Market. The team will be based in London, with plans to build out underwriting, exposure management, and claims capabilities, supporting the Crisis Solutions business and SiriusPoint’s next phase of growth within the London Market Specialty division.
